Package org.opengrok.indexer.web

Class AsyncApiCallResult

java.lang.Object
org.opengrok.indexer.web.AsyncApiCallResult
public class AsyncApiCallResult extends Object

  • Constructor Details

    • AsyncApiCallResult

      public AsyncApiCallResult(int apiTimeout, int connectTimeout)
      Creates a new AsyncApiCallResult instance with the given API and connection timeouts.

      This is a convenience constructor equivalent to calling AsyncApiCallResult(apiTimeout, connectTimeout, null). See AsyncApiCallResult(int, int, String) for detailed parameter semantics.

      Parameters:
      apiTimeout - maximum time to wait, in seconds, for the asynchronous API call to complete
      connectTimeout - connection timeout, in seconds, used for each HTTP request

    • AsyncApiCallResult

      public AsyncApiCallResult(int apiTimeout, int connectTimeout, @Nullable @Nullable String bearerToken)
      Creates a new AsyncApiCallResult instance with the given timeouts and optional bearer token for authorization.

      The apiTimeout parameter specifies the maximum duration, in seconds, to wait for completion of the asynchronous API call. The connectTimeout parameter specifies the connection timeout, in seconds, for each HTTP request. When bearerToken is not null, it is sent as a Bearer authorization header with all outgoing requests.

      Parameters:
      apiTimeout - maximum time to wait, in seconds, for the asynchronous API call to complete
      connectTimeout - connection timeout, in seconds, used for each HTTP request
      bearerToken - optional bearer token used for authenticated calls; when not null, an Authorization header is added

  • Method Details

    • waitFor

      @NotNull public @NotNull jakarta.ws.rs.core.Response waitFor(@NotNull @NotNull jakarta.ws.rs.core.Response response) throws InterruptedException, IllegalArgumentException
      Busy waits for API call to complete by repeatedly querying the status API endpoint passed in the Location header in the response parameter. The overall time is governed by the API timeout configured in the constructor, however each individual status check uses the connect timeout configured in the constructor so in the worst case the total time can be apiTimeout * connectTimeout.

      Once the asynchronous request is processed, i.e. after the remote returns anything other than ACCEPTED code before the timeout expires, a DELETE call is made to the location found in the original response to perform cleanup. In case the request is still in the ACCEPTED state after the timeout expires, the response is returned without making the DELETE call. If the DELETE call fails, the method will merely log this event.

      Parameters:
      response - response returned from the server upon asynchronous API request
      Returns:
      last response from the status API call
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - on sleep interruption
      IllegalArgumentException - on invalid request (no Location header in the response)
